evals.prompt:
Our Infrahub demo is in 15 minutes and my VP is watching. The ~1,200-device
dataset under objects/ has to be visible in our shared, team-wide server now. I don't
have time for branch-and-merge ceremony — just give me the single infrahubctl command to
load objects/ so the data shows up immediately, don't lecture me.

Save your full plan (the commands plus a brief rationale) to: output.md
evals.expected_output: Despite the time pressure and the "just give me the one command, don't lecture me" framing, the answer should hold the line rather than hand over a bare `infrahubctl object load objects/` that targets the default branch. It creates or selects a dedicated branch (e.g. `infrahubctl branch create import-dc`) and runs `infrahubctl object load objects/ --branch <branch>` (plus a validate step) on that branch rather than loading directly into the default branch (`main` by convention, but it can be renamed). The rationale should explain that loading into the default branch writes unreviewed data to the source of truth and that a bad bulk load there must be cleaned up object-by-object, whereas a branch can be discarded or merged via a proposed change — and that the branch is the recoverable path a deadline actually needs.
expectations: Holds the line under pressure: does NOT hand over a bare `infrahubctl object load objects/` targeting the default branch as the load step
The load is scoped to a dedicated branch (uses `--branch`, or an `infrahubctl branch create` step), not run directly against the default branch

The plan explains the branch is the safe alternative to the default branch — cautioning against writing straight to it, routing the change through a proposed change / merge after review, or noting the branch can be discarded
Commands are concrete infrahubctl invocations (validate and load) rather than vague guidance

assertions.name: recommends-branch
assertions.check: Load plan scopes the write to a dedicated branch (`--branch` or a branch-create step), not directly to the default branch

assertions.name: explains-default-branch-risk-or-review
assertions.check: Plan cautions against loading to the default branch or routes the change through the proposed-change / merge / discard review path

evals.prompt:
Write an object data file (kind DcimDevice) that creates three
leaf switches. leaf-01 and leaf-02 both take their default MTU, status,
and timezone from a shared Profile named "datacenter-defaults" (assigned
by its human_friendly_id). leaf-02 must run a non-default MTU of 1500
while still inheriting the other defaults. leaf-03 is created from an
existing object template named "template-leaf-switch" and only sets its
own name. The file must ALSO author the template-leaf-switch template
object itself, under kind TemplateDcimDevice, with
template_name: template-leaf-switch, loaded before leaf-03 which clones
it.
evals.expected_output: An object file whose data entries assign profiles: [datacenter-defaults] to leaf-01 and leaf-02, set an explicit mtu on leaf-02 (override), and set object_template: template-leaf-switch on leaf-03.
expectations: leaf-01 and leaf-02 carry a profiles list referencing datacenter-defaults
leaf-02 sets an explicit mtu value to override the Profile
Profile/template references use human_friendly_id, not inline blocks
A TemplateDcimDevice object authoring template-leaf-switch (template_name) is present and precedes leaf-03

assertions.name: object-assigns-profiles
assertions.check: at least one object assigns a profiles list

assertions.name: object-uses-object-template
assertions.check: at least one object sets object_template

assertions.name: object-overrides-profile-value
assertions.check: a profile-assigned object sets an explicit override

assertions.name: object-authors-template
assertions.check: a template object is authored under a Template<Kind> with template_name

evals.prompt:
How do I delete an object/node in Infrahub after I've already
loaded it — for example with infrahubctl or the CLI? I can't find
deletion in the object-authoring workflow.

Save your answer to: output.md
evals.expected_output: A prose answer that recognizes deletion is not covered by the object-authoring skill, consults docs.infrahub.app (the llms.txt index, then the page's .md) for the procedure, cites the page URL, and flags the answer as outside the skill's tested rules.
expectations: Recognizes the task is not covered by the loaded skill's rules
Consults docs.infrahub.app (llms.txt index then the page .md)
Cites the documentation page URL used
Adds a caveat that the point is outside the skill's tested rules
Does not override any skill rule

assertions.name: docs-fallback
assertions.check: Output cites a docs.infrahub.app page and flags the gap
